metadata.name: rouge_reference_answers
metadata.description: Golden dataset with high-quality reference answers for ROUGE score evaluation

queries.query: What is the default chunk size in SecondBrain?
queries.reference_answer: The default chunk size in SecondBrain is 4096 tokens. This configuration parameter controls how documents are split into smaller pieces for embedding and vector search.

queries.query: How do I configure MongoDB connection URI?
queries.reference_answer: To configure the MongoDB connection URI, set the SECONDBRAIN_MONGO_URI environment variable to your MongoDB connection string. This is the primary method for specifying database connectivity.
Logging is controlled by two environment variables: SECONDBRAIN_LOG_LEVEL for verbosity (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) and SECONDBRAIN_LOG_FORMAT for output format (pretty or json).

queries.query: How to enable circuit breaker protection?
queries.reference_answer: Enable circuit breaker protection by setting the SECONDBRAIN_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED environment variable to true. This provides automatic failure handling and self-recovery capabilities.

queries.query: What is the default top-k for search results?
queries.reference_answer: The default top-k value is 5, meaning the search returns the 5 most relevant results by default. This can be adjusted via command-line options or configuration.

queries.query: What is the default embedding model?
queries.reference_answer: The default embedding model is all-MiniLM-L6-v2 from the sentence-transformers library. This model provides a good balance between speed and accuracy for semantic search.

queries.query: What is the default chunk overlap value?
queries.reference_answer: The default chunk overlap is 256 tokens. This overlap preserves context between adjacent chunks and improves retrieval quality for queries spanning multiple chunks.

queries.query: What document formats are supported for ingestion?
queries.reference_answer: SecondBrain supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown, images, and audio files. The Ingestor class uses Docling for multi-format document parsing.

Semantic search works by converting queries and documents into embedding vectors using sentence-transformers, then performing vector similarity search in MongoDB using cosine similarity to find the most relevant results.

queries.query: What is the purpose of the Ingestor class?
queries.reference_answer: The Ingestor class handles multi-format document parsing and chunking. It processes various file formats, splits documents into manageable chunks, and prepares them for embedding and storage.

queries.query: How does the circuit breaker work?
queries.reference_answer: The circuit breaker automatically monitors service health and handles failures. When errors exceed a threshold, it opens the circuit to prevent cascading failures, then attempts automatic recovery after a cooldown period.

queries.query: What are the main components of SecondBrain architecture?
queries.reference_answer: The main components are: CLI layer for commands, Document Ingestor for parsing and chunking, Embedding Engine for vector generation, MongoDB for vector storage, and Searcher for semantic retrieval. These work together in a pipeline architecture.
